description: Shared brand, residue, pricing, and offer-surface rules for starter-template commerce families that consume next-core.css brand tokens.

brand_tokens.note: Starter commerce pages consume --brand--* tokens from next-core.css. Campaign-namespace tokens style custom source markup only; they never restyle SDK commerce surfaces unless mapped into --brand--* overrides.

logo.rule: Campaign brand logo must replace the starter next-logo.png on commerce pages unless the CampaignSpec store branding intentionally keeps it.

payment_chrome.rule: Remove unless the method is listed in CampaignSpec available_payment_methods / available_express_payment_methods. Template chrome outside the SDK payment-method include counts as implied payment-method residue.
payment_chrome.note: paypal intentionally has two selectors. QA fails if any selector for an unsupported method remains visible.

demo_assets.rule: Template demo placeholder assets (1x1 spacer SVGs, repeated benefit icons, starter imagery) must be re-skinned to the campaign's real assets before launch. Surviving demo assets are a warning, not a blocker: the agent should replace them rather than ship template placeholders. Declared per family; arrays replace on extends, so a family lists its own demo-asset set.

placeholder_text_residue.rule: Rendered campaign output must not contain literal template placeholder copy. Every term is replaced from the CampaignSpec/design before QA; QA fails (blocker) on any match in visible page text, regardless of any theme-gate waiver. Matched on word boundaries, case-insensitive. Scope is governed by page_types: the blocker applies only to the listed campaign funnel page types (generic, non-funnel pages whose type is not listed are not checked). Narrow page_types to exempt a page type where placeholder copy is acceptable.

pricing_surfaces.rule: Pricing surfaces render via mode-driven partial arguments, never via campaign CSS that hides price rows. Campaign stylesheets must not apply display:none to price wrappers or SDK price displays.
pricing_surfaces.same_price_strike_rule: If no offer, voucher, compare-at value, or code-discounted post-checkout state governs an order bump, upsell, or downsell, render full_price only; do not show identical struck-through and current prices.

upsell.note: A full_price upsell shows exactly one visible price row adjacent to the accept control. Zero visible price rows on an upsell/downsell offer is a blocker.

initial_hint_state.rule: Empty checkout fields must have a legible initial hint state: either visible labels, placeholder-style hints, or both. Accessible-only labels are not sufficient if the visible placeholder/hint text is stripped from empty inputs.

exit_pop.rule: If an exit-intent include or default widget ships without CampaignSpec checkout exit_intent or promo_code_input behavior, strip it or record a hard warning. If present, it must apply the mapped offer through the SDK/API coupon path and use cart.hasCoupon("CODE") style conditionals for applied-state presentation.
